title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security
description: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Learners cumulatively produce a bounded Git automation and trust policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
